title: epipactis helleborine subsp. troodi
intro: Epipactis helleborine subsp. troodi, also known as Troöd's Helleborine or Cyprus Helleborine, is a rare and endangered subspecies of orchid native to Cyprus. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, Epipactis helleborine subsp. troodi is valued for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and soothing respiratory problems, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, Epipactis helleborine subsp. troodi is often used to create infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be applied topically to wounds or taken internally to promote overall well-being.
